description:
Hey ,

I noticed a few discrepancies between Gould Injury Law’s GBP listings and the phone numbers on their website. Before reaching out to the client, can you please confirm whether we already have this information or access to any call tracking/local number details?

1. New Haven and Waterford GBPs share the same primary GBP phone number.

- Can you confirm which numbers are correct for each location (New Haven, Waterbury, Hartford), and/or if we have unique numbers?

For proper attribution and NAP consistency, each GBP should have its own unique local primary number and adhere to the GBP phone number structure as outlined below.
 

Ideal GBP Phone Number Structure 

 
PRIMARY - GBP Call Tracking Number (unique to that GBP) 
- If no GBP call tracking, use local number.

- Number used on citation listings 
- Number should match the phone number on site (the number associated with the NAP in the footer and/or alongside the location's map embeds, etc.) in order to correlate the website and GBP for Google.

SETTLEMENT $1,100,000

Joseph was a retired veteran but couldn’t keep himself busy enough so he took a job as a handyman at a local garage. He had been working there for a few years keeping the garage clean, moving cars around, and generally helping out with keeping the facility running smooth. One morning he was using a backpack blower to clean out one of the bays and pressed the button to close the bay door. As the steel door closed, it caught the back pack and came down on his neck gradually pushing him to the floor. Joseph was killed when the door closed entirely on him breaking his neck. The garage door failed to have a simple safety cut off that would have sensed him blocking the door, similar to what one might find in a residential garage door. That defect cost Joseph his life.

title: Some more workers comp case studies...
description:
James Harris had been employed by a maintenance company for many years when one afternoon at work his back gave out on him after moving a heavy hose. After 5 months of fighting, his employer finally agreed to pay for his back surgery which was very successful. With a few months of rehabilitation, James felt much better but was forced to find a new, less physically taxing job. This Worker’s Compensation case was fully settled in under 2 years for $92,000.



Michael Mayor worked a major grocery store for many years. During that time he sustained two hernias which his doctor told him was due to him lifting and tossing frozen turkeys at work. His employer fought very aggressively arguing that hernias are not caused by trauma or lifting and occur naturally to anyone. Just before a formal hearing was to occur, the grocery store agreed to settle this claim for $60,000. This settlement was notable not just for the amount, but because hernias are incredibly difficult to classify as a work injury but also because Michael had no long term disability from his.

Hartford, CT-Slip and Fall
description:
For this site: https://gouldinjurylaw.com/about-the-firm/

Word count: 500 words

KEYWORDS--Navigate to this link to use this tool to target the keywords for this page: https://app.surferseo.com/drafts/s/CpoGoPEILnAVY8vvplNqsWH9W69lyvdV

In lieu of extensive client style notes, please note the following: 
  • He likes to be known as aggressive and fast. We can use the words fast, and faster, but not fastest. He is going to be rebranding to 888-WIN-FAST in a few years so he wants to use that liberally.
  • In fact, he likes "fast/faster" to be well-distributed throughout the content quite a bit. If your page is about 1000-1500 words and you have a count of "fast" only 6 times, you might up it to 10 times.
  • Always include "fast/faster" in the first 1-2 paragraphs of your page.
  • The current site's case page content is VERY readable--very low grade on Hemingway App. You don't have to go quite this low, but try to keep sentences uncomplicated and to use words that are easy to understand. Greatly limit your pink (and yellow) sentences. 
  • Be very relatable and down to earth in tone and style. (Review the readability guidelines in the style guide!!)
  • Put yourself in the client's shoes for this one. Notice how this page does this with imaginative imagery: https://gouldinjurylaw.com/road-accidents/. Don't go overboard with this, though. I feel like this page overdo it. But it's insight into how the firm wants to relate to their clients. Take this page for example: https://gouldinjurylaw.com/bike-accidents/. 
  • IMPORTANT--Something I find lacking (or kind of illogically organized) in our content sometimes is focusing on problems and solutions. From the top of the page or section, identify and target the problem, the pain point, that is bothering the victim for that case type. Then propose a solution and explain why the firm can provide that solution. Use this problem-solution approach to sort your content logically. 
  • As always, write in a way that will move the reader to action. I only want to highlight this so we remember that our aim to persuade the reader. This is why we must use relatable language that ANYONE will understand and relate to.

  • He likes to be known as aggressive and fast. We can use the words fast, and faster, but not fastest. He is going to be rebranding to 888-WIN-FAST in a few years so he wants to use that liberally.
  • The current site's case page content is VERY readable--very low grade on Hemingway App. You don't have to go quite this low, but try to keep sentences uncomplicated and to use words that are easy to understand. Greatly limit your pink (and yellow) sentences. 
  • Be very relatable and down to earth in tone and style. (Review the readability guidelines in the style guide!!)
  • Put yourself in the client's shoes for this one. Notice how this page does this with imaginative imagery: https://gouldinjurylaw.com/road-accidents/. Don't go overboard with this, though. I feel like this page overdo it. But it's insight into how the firm wants to relate to their clients. Take this page for example: https://gouldinjurylaw.com/bike-accidents/. 
  • IMPORTANT--Something I find lacking (or kind of illogically organized) in our content sometimes is focusing on problems and solutions. From the top of the page or section, identify and target the problem, the pain point, that is bothering the victim for that case type. Then propose a solution and explain why the firm can provide that solution. Use this problem-solution approach to sort your content logically. 
  • As always, write in a way that will move the reader to action. I only want to highlight this so we remember that our aim to persuade the reader. This is why we must use relatable language that ANYONE will understand and relate to.

title: Create and Send Proposal -
description:
,

I'm pasting part of an email I sent to Bob last November. Bob, is opting in for option 2 "$5,000 per month", SEO for his two locations, going after General PI terms first.

Bob's email address: rgould@gouldinjurylaw.com

"Currently, Google has two locations for your law firm: 
One in Hartford, and the other in New Haven. 
Does your law firm operate out of both locations?

If so, we will want to optimize both locations, but in the beginning in order to maximize budget and quickest path to lead generation, we will work on growing your website and Hartford Google My business listing. 

 Personal injury is one of the most competitive markets in Google search optimization and Google search paid advertising. However, Your market is in the medium competition scale, and does not require the budget of someone in a Boston type market. 

This means with the right focus and resources, we can get some quick ranking wins in the Personal injury keyword buckets. Keywords like: 
Hartford personal injury lawyer
personal injury lawyer hartford ct 
personal injury lawyer
injury lawyer ct
injury lawyers

Your website will need work no matter what agency helps you in your SEO journey. We will need to address the many domain names pointing to your website, and decide on a brand name to use going forward: Gould law group (website url) or Gould injury law (associated with GMB and directory sites). 

I want you to know, I'm sensitive to costs, and dont want to spend money or time on activities that dont get us towards the results we are after. Also, SEO is something you want to commit to for at least six months. Of course with us, you can cancel with a 30 day paid notice. 

With your campaign, we will spend the first month addressing your website and Google my business foundational needs. We wont get far in our ranking efforts, if our foundation is poor. Month 2, we will begin the proactive ranking efforts, which will put us in month 3-4 before we have solid page one results. This doesn't mean we won't achieve page one rankings before that, I'm trying to set proper expectations.

To keep this investment cost effective, I would recommend we focus our efforts towards Personal injury rankings, and one location first. 

Once we have solid rankings for the website and the first location, we can look to reallocate resources, towards the secondary location. 

Option 1 - For this plan, the monthly investment would be around $3,500 pm. 

Option 2 - If you want to invest into the secondary location from the start, we would need an additional $1,500 pm = $5,000pm 

(please keep these prices between us, as our single location seo campaigns start at $5k pm, we are giving you a discount because you are a client referral)

"
*** important to add in the proposal ***

Apart of our marketing campaign will be: 
  • 1st 30 days - Decision on Website - keep and optimize, take the content and add it to a new CMS (rebuild). We are rolling out with his brand name and his website: Gouldinjurylaw.com Gould Injury law. 
  • We will need to update ALL legal & review directories, as well as his social media, and optimize his Youtube channel and videos. 
  • We will need to do the same with his 2 GMB listings. 
  • Bob, made it clear that he wants his branding "888-WIN-FAST" to be front and centered in all that we do. 
  • Create a list of his directory logins, so we can share with Bob
